name: Mon Laferte
description:
'''Norma Monserrat Bustamante''' '''Laferte''' (born 2 May 1983), known as '''Mon Laferte''', is a Chilean and Mexican singer-songwriter. Recognized for her versatility, her musical style spans a wide array of genres, including [[Pop music|pop]], [[Alternative music|alternative]], [[bolero]], [[cumbia]], and [[Salsa music|salsa]]. She rose to international prominence in the [[2010s]], particularly for her melodramatic vocals and theatrical stage presence. In 2025, [[Billboard (magazine)|''Billboard'']] named her one of the best female Latin pop artists of all time.

With over 4.8 million [[Album-equivalent unit|album-equivalent units]] sold in Mexico, Laferte is the best-selling Chilean artist of the digital era. Her most successful releases, including the albums ''[[Mon Laferte, Vol. 1]]'' (2015) and ''[[La Trenza]]'' (2017), and the singles "[[Tu falta de querer|Tu Falta De Querer]]," "[[Amárrame]]," and "[[Mi Buen Amor (Mon Laferte song)|Mi Buen Amor]]," have been certified diamond or higher by [[AMPROFON]]. Her accolades include five [[Latin Grammy Awards]]—the most for any Chilean performer—a [[Musa Awards|Musa Award]], two [[MTV Europe Music Awards]], and three [[Grammy Awards|Grammy Award]] nominations.
